mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically boosting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 7.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 7.0, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and residential housing starts signals synchronized real-sector expansion, which amplifies corporate revenue growth expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in out-of-sample walk-forward tests, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression of 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, a contraction in real consumer credit signals tightening financial conditions that suppress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when RSAFS declines during periods of rising FEDFUNDS, or the conditional IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate earnings per share and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days without requiring specific threshold crossings.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between quarterly GDPC1 growth and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.10 over any 5-year window, or forward returns show no directional bias following positive GDP surprises.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 2.5 percent year-over-year, inflation expectations embed into longer-term discount rates, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL annualized growth exceeds 2.5 percent, or the signal exhibits no predictive rank correlation over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong employment growth and weakening consumer credit indicates a credit-constrained labor market that precedes a demand shock, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in walk-forward tests, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of peak industrial capacity utilization, a sudden spike in the unemployment rate signals a supply-demand mismatch that erodes profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline when UNRATE spikes during high INDPRO periods, or the conditional forward return distribution shows no significant negative skew over a 2-year rolling window.
